summary: Tested all 6 requirements for the Payroll Publish feature (Iteration 19). Backend: 16/16 tests pass. Frontend: all UI elements verified via Playwright. May 2026 salary slips are published (38 records all 'processed'). Period filter is dynamic and includes June/July 2026. Publish Payslips button is present with indigo color.

minor.issue: publishPayslips() in Payroll.js checks `records.filter(r => r.period === p && r.status === 'draft')` where `records` is filtered by `filterPeriod`. If filterPeriod is set to a different period than the toolbar month, the local draftCount will be 0 even when drafts exist, showing a false 'No draft payslips' alert. Backend still executes correctly — it's only the front-end guard that's wrong.

test_results.publish_requires_admin_role: PASS — employee role gets 403, unauthenticated gets 401/403
test_results.summary_card_for_may_2026: PASS — shows Total Net ₹7,82,217 (38 emp), Deductions ₹45,254, LOP Days 215, Employer ₹51,004, CTC ₹9,01,321
test_results.all_records_show_processed_status: PASS — all 38 May 2026 rows show 'processed' badge
test_results.employee_draft_gating: PASS — RMF0006 (employee role) cannot see their April 2026 draft payslip
test_results.employee_july_gating: PASS — employee cannot see July 2026 payslips (current month not ended)
test_results.employee_no_draft_records: PASS — all records visible to employee are processed/paid
test_results.employee_own_records_only: PASS — employee only sees their own payroll
test_results.admin_june_july_query: PASS — admin can query 2026-06 and 2026-07 periods without error

action_items: LOW: Fix publishPayslips() frontend guard — use `filterPeriod` state or reset filter before counting drafts. The check `records.filter(r => r.period === p && r.status === 'draft')` can return 0 falsely when `filterPeriod` is set to a different period than the toolbar. The backend works correctly; only the pre-flight alert can be misleading.
critical_code_review_comments: Minor: publishPayslips() in Payroll.js uses local `records` state to count drafts, but `records` may be filtered to a different period. Edge case: user sets filterPeriod=2026-04 but toolbar is May — clicking Publish shows 'No draft payslips' even if May has drafts. Fix: either unset filterPeriod before count, or skip local count and let backend respond.
Design OK: periodOptions useMemo correctly uses `new Date()` for dynamic range — no hardcoded end year.

test_credentials.note: RMF0002 login returns 'Invalid username or password' — appears deactivated or password changed. RMF0006 confirmed working.

seed_data_creation: None. Used existing data (37 published May 2026 records + 1 more = 38 total processed records).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Payroll Publish feature requirements verified. May 2026 has 38 processed records (0 draft). Period filter is dynamic up to current month (July 2026). Publish Payslips button with indigo color works. Employee gating: draft = invisible, current-month = invisible, past+processed = visible. RMF0006 (employee) works for testing; RMF0002 is inactive. One low-priority frontend UX issue: publishPayslips() local draft count can give false 'No draft payslips' alert when filterPeriod differs from toolbar period.

publishPayslips_frontend_guard.root_cause: const draftCount = records.filter(r => r.period === p && r.status === 'draft').length — `records` is the currently loaded state which is filtered by `filterPeriod`. If filterPeriod ≠ toolbar period, draftCount will be 0 causing premature bail-out.
publishPayslips_frontend_guard.impact: Low — backend is unaffected. Only pre-flight UI alert can fire incorrectly.
publishPayslips_frontend_guard.fix: Either call backend directly without local count check, or do `const draftCount = records.filter(r => r.period === p && r.status === 'draft').length` only when `!filterPeriod || filterPeriod === p`, else skip the guard.
